explicit_text: I was born and raised in Ethiopia, a country rich in culture and tradition. My family still lives there, including my father Nasir Ahmed and mother Shetu Mohammed. Leaving Ethiopia was difficult because I left behind my brothers, sisters, and close friends. Ethiopia is where my roots are, especially connected to the Gurage ethnic group I belong to.

explicit_text: I grew up in Adama, also known as Nazret, which is a beautiful city in central Ethiopia southeast of Addis Ababa. The city is surrounded by mountains and changes with the seasons, green in winter and brown in summer. My family and I lived together on the same property with my brother's family nearby. Adama holds many of my childhood memories.

explicit_text: Addis Ababa is the capital city of Ethiopia, located northwest of Adama where I grew up. explicit_text: In 2007, I traveled to Qatar and stayed there for four years. It was my first experience living outside Ethiopia, and it prepared me for my later move to the United States. Qatar was an important chapter in my journey before settling in America.

explicit_text: I came to the United States in 2011 after living in Qatar. My first stop was Ohio, where I stayed for 20 days before moving to Dallas, Texas. The U.S. has been my home since then, and I have lived in different states like Texas and Minnesota while building a new life away from my family in Ethiopia.

explicit_text: My family is from the Gurage ethnic group in Ethiopia, and I am proud of that heritage. Growing up, I learned a lot about Gurage culture from my father and aunt, including traditions around marriage and family gatherings. The Gurage people have a close-knit community, and I cherish the memories of our traditional foods and celebrations.

explicit_text: Kitfo is one of the traditional foods from my Gurage culture that I always remember fondly. It was a staple at family gatherings where we would eat together and have fun. Even now, I miss sharing kitfo with my family back in Ethiopia.
implicit_text: One of the traditional foods from my ethnic culture that I always remember fondly is a spiced raw beef dish. It was a staple at family gatherings where we would eat together and have fun. Even now, I miss sharing this dish with my family back home.

explicit_text: Kocho is another traditional dish from my Gurage heritage that I grew up eating. It was always part of our family meals during celebrations and everyday life. Kocho reminds me of home and the close family ties we shared in Ethiopia.
implicit_text: Another traditional dish from my heritage that I grew up eating is made from fermented enset. It was always part of our family meals during celebrations and everyday life. This dish reminds me of home and the close family ties we shared.

explicit_text: Gomen is a traditional vegetable dish from my Gurage culture that I remember well. It was often served alongside other foods like kitfo and kocho during family gatherings. Gomen brings back warm memories of my childhood and the strong bonds within my family.
